Comparison

SpiniX vs TurboPush

TurboPush is the budget option: AI-designed digital stamp cards in Apple and Google Wallet from $5.99/month. SpiniX is the engagement option: gamified spin wheel with review generation and email automation from €29/month. Different price points, different capabilities.

SpiniX vs TurboPush: the short version

SpiniX vs TurboPush: feature comparison

Starting price €29/mo
Mid-tier price €59/mo
Top-tier price €99/mo
Apple/Google Wallet Yes
POS required No
Core loyalty mechanic Spin wheel (variable rewards)
AI card design No (manual setup)
AI message generation No
Push notifications Unlimited (Wallet push)
Google review generation Automatic flow (33% conversion)
Email marketing Automated 6-email sequence
WhatsApp integration Yes (SEA/UK markets)
Gamification Core mechanic on every scan
Guest database Email, phone, visit history
Card designs per plan Customizable wheel (all plans)
Analytics Dashboard + Telegram alerts
Setup speed 10 minutes (manual config)
Multi-location Per-location wheels
Free trial 14 days

SpiniX vs TurboPush: where each platform wins

TurboPush wins: lowest possible price

$5.99/month is hard to argue with. If you're a small cafe doing 20-30 covers per day and you just want a digital stamp card that lives in Apple Wallet, TurboPush delivers that for less than the cost of a coffee per week. No other platform in this category matches that price point.

TurboPush wins: AI-powered setup speed

TurboPush's AI generates a branded loyalty card design in 30 seconds. No design skills needed. AI also writes push notification messages for you. SpiniX requires manual wheel configuration (takes about 10 minutes). If zero-effort setup matters, TurboPush is faster.

SpiniX wins: gamification drives higher engagement

TurboPush digitizes the stamp card. SpiniX replaces it with a spin wheel. The psychology difference is significant: stamp cards have 20-30% completion rates with 73% member inactivity. Variable rewards (the spin) produce 3x more dopamine and maintain engagement because every visit feels rewarding, not just the 10th one.

SpiniX wins: review generation pays for itself

This is the biggest differentiator. SpiniX generates Google reviews automatically (33% conversion rate). TurboPush doesn't touch reviews at all. A single 5-star Google review influences 8-12 potential customers. At 50 covers/day with 46% scan rate and 33% review rate, that's roughly 7-8 reviews per day. The revenue impact of those reviews easily covers the €29/month subscription.

SpiniX wins: email automation that drives returns

TurboPush has no email capability. SpiniX runs a 6-email automated sequence: welcome, expiry reminder, review request, return nudge, birthday, and win-back. Email marketing delivers $42 ROI per dollar in hospitality. Without email, TurboPush relies entirely on push notifications to bring guests back, and the Basic plan limits those to 500/month.

SpiniX wins: complete guest database

SpiniX captures email, phone (optional), visit history, spin history, and review status for every guest. This data is exportable and usable across marketing channels. TurboPush tracks card holders but doesn't build a structured guest database you can leverage beyond their platform.

SpiniX vs TurboPush: FAQ

Is TurboPush really $5.99/month?

Yes. The Basic plan is $5.99/month with unlimited digital cards, 500 push notifications per month, and 2 card designs. It's a legitimate product at that price. The limitation is what's not included: no email, no reviews, no gamification, no guest database export.

Can TurboPush generate Google reviews?

No. TurboPush focuses exclusively on digital loyalty cards and push notifications. There's no review generation feature. To collect reviews, you'd need a separate tool (typically $50-200/month), which often costs more than the TurboPush subscription itself.

Is 500 push notifications per month enough?

Depends on your database size. With 100 Wallet card holders, that's 5 messages per person per month, enough for weekly updates. With 500+ card holders, you'd hit the limit sending just one message. Most growing businesses outgrow the Basic plan within 3-6 months.

Can I switch from TurboPush to SpiniX later?

Yes, but you can't migrate Wallet passes between platforms. Your guests would need to scan a new SpiniX QR code to join. The transition works well if you place new QR codes alongside old ones. Within 2-3 weeks, most active guests will have both.

Which is better for a cafe?

Small cafe (under 30 covers/day): TurboPush. The "buy 9, get 10th free" stamp card works well for daily coffee habits, and $5.99/month keeps costs minimal. Busy cafe (50+ covers/day): SpiniX. At that volume, the gamification and review generation provide significantly more value than the cost difference.

Ready for loyalty that does more than stamp cards?

Try SpiniX free for 14 days. Gamification, reviews, and email automation included. See the difference in engagement within the first week.
